summary:
The process begins with evaluating risks and recommending dietary changes; if necessary, restorations are performed after non-invasive methods have been exhausted or proven ineffective for managing carious lesions across teeth 51-52 and 61-62. \boxed{B}
The approach prioritizes initial assessments and prevention over immediate intervention, ensuring that more aggressive steps like fillings occur only when other options fall short. This reflects current best-practice standards in dentistry aimed at preserving natural tooth structure while effectively treating cavities.
